---
pagetitle: "RECAST — Replication and Extension with Causal AI Statistical Toolkit"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}

::: {.recast-title}
[RECAST]{.recast-wordmark}

**R**eplication and **E**xtension with **C**ausal **A**I **S**tatistical **T**oolkit
:::

## Replicate a result. Extend it with causal ML. Referee it. Report it honestly.

RECAST takes a published econometrics paper and its replication data, reproduces
the original result, extends it with Double/Debiased Machine Learning and generic
ML for heterogeneity, runs the extension through a structured AI peer review, and
writes an honest report — with the same code path every time.

::: {.hero-buttons}
[How it works](methodology.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[See the papers](papers.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg}
:::
:::

::: {.define-block}
**RECAST** *(verb)* — to take an econometrics paper's headline result, **re-cast**
it through a flexible, machine-learning control function: keep the original
identification, relax the functional form, quantify any heterogeneity, and let an
adversarial AI referee decide whether the new estimate can be trusted.
:::

## How it works

RECAST is a four-step pipeline. Each step is a separate agent that communicates
only through files, so every number on this site is traceable to an artifact.

::: {.pipeline-steps}
::: {.step}
[1]{.step-n}
### Replicate
Reproduce the original OLS/IV result *coefficient by coefficient*, at the paper's
own standard-error convention. A two-regime test grades each headline coefficient
**success / partial / failure** — and a failed replication halts the pipeline
before any ML runs.
:::
::: {.step}
[2]{.step-n}
### Extend
Swap the parametric first stage for cross-fitted machine learning (DoubleML:
PLR / IRM / PLIV), keeping the paper's *inherited* conditioning set. Seven
learners, median aggregation, B&N-adjusted SEs; generic ML (BLP / GATES / CLAN)
for heterogeneity when the treatment is binary.
:::
::: {.step}
[3]{.step-n}
### Review
Two isolated AI referees — a general referee (identification + replication
integrity) and a strict DML-technical referee (a 19-point canonical audit, sole
authority to *block*) — review the extension, not the original paper. A synthesis
referee quality-controls them.
:::
::: {.step}
[4]{.step-n}
### Report
A gap table (original | ours | benchmark | difference), an estimand statement, and
a plain-English verdict that says what the machine learning did **and did not**
add. The benchmark is hidden from the estimator until the results are frozen.
:::
:::

[Read the methodology in depth →](methodology.qmd)

## Does it work? The evidence

RECAST has been run end-to-end on six published papers spanning four
identification strategies — including two difference-in-differences designs. The
results are the point of the site — including the ones that didn't come out clean.

::: {.features-grid}
::: {.feature}
**Reproduces hand-built work, bit-for-bit**
<p>On Nunn &amp; Trefler (2010), the automated pipeline matched a hand-built
replication on all 21 DML cells with zero numerical drift, and matched the
Baiardi–Naghi benchmark 21/21.</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Reports honest divergence**
<p>On a teacher-training RCT, the null average effect and the heterogeneity
*shape* reproduced — but the headline heterogeneity slope diverged from the
benchmark (0.20 vs 0.65). RECAST flags it as implementation sensitivity, not a win.</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Knows its limits**
<p>On Ashraf &amp; Galor (2013), the quadratic IV *hump* replicated exactly
(optimal point 0.690) — but a linear PLIV can't represent a hump, so the extension
is labeled functional-form-limited rather than passed off as the effect.</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Enforces control discipline**
<p>On Djankov et al. (2010), a screen flagged 6 of 12 "kitchen-sink" controls as
plausible bad controls; the inherited set stays the headline and the resulting
sensitivity is disclosed (−0.200 → −0.230).</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Catches its own bugs**
<p>The test battery surfaced and fixed real framework bugs — and a deliberate
fault-injection halts on a bad replication instead of fabricating a result.</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Replicate, or don't extend**
<p>The replication gate is blocking. RECAST will not run a causal-ML extension
unless it has the paper <em>and</em> has reproduced a <em>published</em> number
within reasonable deviation. On the open-access Trust/FinTech paper it reproduces
Table 5's triple difference within ~2% and passes; a missing paper or a result
with no published number to match is <code>BLOCKED</code>, not waved through.</p>
:::
::: {.feature}
**Extends to difference-in-differences**
<p>On a continuous-treatment DiD (FinTech adoption after the Wells-Fargo scandal)
the router picks PLR on the two-way-FE panel and the effect survives DML; on a
staggered binary DiD (faith-based initiatives) it correctly tries
<code>DoubleMLDIDMulti</code> and falls back gracefully when 50 states prove too
few — and labels the fallback honestly.</p>
:::
:::

## The papers at a glance

| Paper | Identification | Verdict | Headline |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Nunn & Trefler (2010)](papers/nunn_trefler_2010/) | PLR / OLS | Ready | DML ≈ halves the OLS effect; bit-for-bit regression test |
| [Loyalka et al. (2019)](papers/teacher_training_2019/) | IRM + generic ML | Ready | ATE null reproduces; β₂ heterogeneity diverges (honest) |
| [Ashraf & Galor (2013)](papers/ashraf_galor_2013/) | PLIV (IV) | Ready | Quadratic hump replicates exactly; PLIV functional-form-limited |
| [Djankov et al. (2010)](papers/djankov_taxes_2010/) | PLR / OLS | Ready | 3/3 exact OLS; control-discipline sensitivity disclosed |
| [Trust / FinTech (2025)](papers/trust_fintech_2025/) | DiD → DID_PLR | Gate PASS | Open-access: published Table 5 triple-diff reproduced within ~2% → gate passes → DML extension (0.014–0.022) |
| [Divine Policy (2025)](papers/divine_policy_2025/) | Staggered DiD | Extension demo | CS estimator infeasible at 50 states → graceful, flagged DID_PLR fallback |

[Browse all papers →](papers.qmd)

RECAST also continuously **scouts** newly published papers with available replication
packages (from the [AEA Replication Tracker](https://paulgp.com/replication-package-db/))
and logs which ones it could process — see [candidate scouting](candidates.qmd).

## We only claim what we tested

RECAST's estimand router covers more designs than have been exercised. The
[coverage page](methodology/coverage.qmd) states plainly which method paths are
validated (PLR, IRM + generic ML, PLIV, difference-in-differences, the failure
gate, control discipline) and which are **not yet tested** (binary-IV → LATE,
multivalued → APOS, large-N staggered DiD). Faithfulness on the tested paths is
not a claim of generality.

[See method-path coverage →](methodology/coverage.qmd)
